message:
perf(sqlite): eliminate write contention from read-only CLI commands

Read-only commands (ready, show, etc.) were triggering SQLITE_BUSY
errors under parallel access because:

1. SqliteStorage::drop performed a WAL checkpoint, turning teardown
   into a write-like operation that contended with other readers.
2. Every open paid the cost of full schema compatibility checks even
   when the database was already at the current schema version.
3. The ready command opened a second database connection instead of
   reusing the one from the startup context.

Fix by removing the WAL checkpoint from Drop, adding a fast path that
reads the SQLite file header to skip schema checks when user_version
is current, reusing pre-opened storage for the ready command, and
guarding the v3 migration rebuild behind an actual column constraint
check. New e2e test validates that 6 parallel readers do not contend.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
